canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, semantic coverage is defined as the degree to which a model-generated text captures the key topics, concepts, and meaning of a reference text. This construct is used to evaluate LLM-generated content, such as literature summaries and compositions, by comparing them against human-written counterparts. The work operationalizes this concept by using "commonly employed semantic similarity metrics and Key Point Recall (KPR)" to calculate the coverage between the generated and human-written context. Within this paper, semantic coverage is assessed alongside factual consistency as a dimension for evaluating generated text.
papers: MPCG: Multi-Round Persona-Conditioned Generation for Modeling the Evolution of Misinformation withLLMs
POINTS-Reader: Distillation-Free Adaptation of Vision-Language Models for Document Conversion
Static or Dynamic: Towards Query-Adaptive Token Selection for Video Question Answering

definitions.text: The degree to which a model-generated text captures the key topics, concepts, and meaning present in a reference text.
definitions.paper: POINTS-Reader: Distillation-Free Adaptation of Vision-Language Models for Document Conversion
